requirements.statement: A shared Knowledge Core provides semantic_search and embedding over Weaviate (vector) + PostgreSQL (structured), and is the single brain all features depend on.
requirements.source_quote: One brain, multiple interfaces. ... All features depend on it.

requirements.statement: EmployeeIntelligenceProfile unifies CV data, skills, feedback summaries, and project history.
requirements.source_quote: Unified model combining: CV data, Skills, Feedback summaries, Project history

requirements.statement: Public RAG chatbot answers client and candidate queries using only retrieved context.
requirements.source_quote: Public-facing AI agent (clients + candidates) ... Generate response via Claude

requirements.statement: Chatbot response time is under 2 seconds.
Chatbot never hallucinates: grounded answers only; abstain when grounding is insufficient.
requirements.source_quote: Grounded answers only (no hallucinations) ... Never hallucinate capabilities ... If unsure -> ask clarification

requirements.statement: Chatbot detects intent (client vs candidate) and routes context retrieval accordingly.
Feedback Intelligence Engine turns peer/manager/self reviews into structured strengths, weaknesses, risks (burnout/conflict), and team-dynamics signals, each with evidence.
requirements.source_quote: Analyze performance feedback and extract: strengths, weaknesses, behavioral signals, risks

requirements.statement: AI Team Assembler analyzes a project then scores candidates and assembles a team (basic matching for MVP).
Team scoring uses behavioral/feedback signals derived from the Feedback Intelligence Engine.
requirements.source_quote: Team selection ignores behavioral data => fail; Data Used: Feedback intelligence

requirements.statement: System is GDPR compliant; sensitive employee data stays in-VPC and is minimized in prompts/logs.
Role-based access control at the API layer AND the retrieval layer (principal -> Weaviate tenant).
Append-only audit logs for every AI decision, keyed by trace_id.
No external exposure of sensitive employee data; public chatbot runs on a PII-free tenant.
requirements.source_quote: No exposure of sensitive employee data externally

requirements.statement: Explainability: every decision outputs why (citations / evidence_ref / rationale).
requirements.source_quote: Every decision must output: Why this answer, Why this person/team

requirements.statement: Frontend provides a public chat widget, an internal feedback dashboard, and an internal team-assembly UI.
requirements.source_quote: Public: Chat widget; Internal: Feedback dashboard; Internal: Team assembly UI

requirements.statement: Team optimization (maximize skill coverage, collaboration probability, delivery success).
requirements.source_quote: Goal: Maximize skill coverage, collaboration probability, delivery success likelihood

shared_interfaces.name: KnowledgeCore.semantic_search
shared_interfaces.signature: semantic_search(query: str, domain_filter: DomainFilter, auth_ctx: AuthContext, k: int = 5) -> list[RetrievedChunk]

shared_interfaces.name: EmployeeIntelligenceProfile
shared_interfaces.signature: dataclass(employee_id, name, cv_text, skills, feedback_summary, project_history, availability)

shared_interfaces.name: EmployeeRepository
shared_interfaces.signature: get(employee_id, auth_ctx) -> EmployeeIntelligenceProfile | None ; list_candidates(auth_ctx, required_skills?) -> list[EmployeeIntelligenceProfile]

shared_interfaces.name: FeedbackIntelligence
shared_interfaces.signature: analyze(reviews, auth_ctx) -> FeedbackAnalysis (PERSISTS by employee_id) ; get(employee_id, auth_ctx) -> FeedbackAnalysis | None

shared_interfaces.name: FeedbackAnalysis
shared_interfaces.signature: dataclass(analysis_id, employee_id, feedback_score[0,1], sentiment[-1,1], strengths, weaknesses, risks, team_dynamics_signals, confidence_score)

shared_interfaces.name: PUBLIC_AUTH_CONTEXT
shared_interfaces.signature: AuthContext(allowed_tenants={'public'}, allowed_domains=PUBLIC_DOMAINS) — /chat/query MUST use exactly this (REQ-013)

shared_interfaces.name: AuditRow
shared_interfaces.signature: dataclass(trace_id, timestamp, principal_id, module, inputs_hash, retrieved_chunk_ids, prompt_version, model_version, output_ref, grounding_score, tenant)

shared_interfaces.name: AuditSink
shared_interfaces.signature: append(row: AuditRow) -> None  (append-only; no update/delete grants)

shared_interfaces.name: accessors
shared_interfaces.signature: get_knowledge_core() ; get_embedding_service() ; get_employee_repository() ; get_feedback_intelligence() ; get_audit_sink() — every module MUST call these (AC-4), not instantiate private impls

acceptance_criteria.description: No hallucination: a substantive RAG answer carries >=1 citation whose source_id resolves to a chunk actually retrieved for the query; insufficient grounding => abstained=true. Proven by citation-resolution + a faithfulness judge (threshold 0.7) at the runtime gate.
Not generic: every feedback item has an evidence_ref pointing at a real input review with a verbatim quote. Enforced as a human/LLM-judge signal, not a denylist.
Behavioral data is used: every team member has a feedback_signal_ref to a real FeedbackIntelligence record; mutating a candidate's feedback changes their match_score/selection (corpus-mutation test).

Shared intelligence is used: every module imports the SAME KnowledgeCore + EmbeddingService (no private duplicate). Runtime proof: mutating the corpus changes chatbot answers (modules route through the one brain, not just import it).
Explainability: every decision emits why (RAG citations, feedback evidence_ref, team rationale + feedback_signal_ref). A decision with no explanation field is invalid.

open_contract_proposals.change: The frozen shared Protocols (KnowledgeCore.semantic_search, EmbeddingService.embed, EmployeeRepository.get/list_candidates, FeedbackIntelligence.analyze/get) are SYNC, but the app stack is async FastAPI/SQLAlchemy. The concrete impls satisfy the sync Protocols (isinstance checks pass) AND additionally expose *_async coroutine variants (get_async, list_candidates_async, analyze_async, get_async; EmbeddingService.embed and KnowledgeCore.semantic_search use sync httpx/weaviate clients so are safe to call from a threadpool). The sync wrappers raise if called from within a running event loop. RECOMMENDATION for Phase-2 worktrees: in async route handlers, await the *_async variants for the DB-backed services (employees/feedback), and call the sync semantic_search/embed via run_in_threadpool (FastAPI sync-route style or starlette.concurrency.run_in_threadpool) to avoid blocking the loop and to keep the chatbot <2s SLA. No signature change to the frozen Protocols is requested; this is an additive convention. File for the contract-lead's awareness.
open_contract_proposals.resolution: Lead ACCEPTED — additive, no Protocol signature change, contract_version stays 1. Phase-2 convention: await the *_async DB methods; call sync semantic_search/embed via starlette.concurrency.run_in_threadpool. Shared credential-agnostic Claude client at app/llm (get_claude_client).

tier1: smoke (health + one happy-path; feature modules hit the REAL knowledge_core)
tier2: LLM/judge grounding & rationale review, gated behind tier1-green, hard attempt cap 5, best-so-far checkpoint

glossary.grounding_threshold: 0.7 — faithfulness/groundedness score in [0,1]; below this OR no supporting retrieved chunk => abstain (abstained=true, ask for clarification, never guess).
glossary.abstain_semantics: abstained=true with a clarification-seeking answer; citations may be empty; counts as a PASS for AC-1 (abstaining is correct, hallucinating is not).
glossary.rbac_tenants: Weaviate tenants: 'public' (PII-free: company/website/case-studies/job-openings only) and 'internal:<org>' (employee profiles, feedback, reviews). The public chatbot is restricted to the 'public' tenant.
glossary.roles: public | candidate | employee | manager | admin. semantic_search requires auth_ctx mapping the principal to allowed tenants/domains; retrieval cannot return data outside them.
glossary.domains: company | case_studies | job_openings | employee_profiles | feedback | project_requirements (per the spec Data Domains table).
glossary.intent_domains: client -> {company, case_studies}; candidate -> {job_openings, company}; unknown -> {company, case_studies, job_openings}. All within the public PII-free set (see shared_interfaces.INTENT_DOMAINS).
glossary.embedding: EMBED_MODEL='sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2', EMBED_DIM=384 — ONE local model across all modules (in-VPC, GDPR; no external embedding API). Pinned in shared_interfaces.py.
glossary.feedback_score_formula: Deterministic [0,1]: start 0.5; +0.08 per strength, -0.06 per weakness, - risk_penalty {low:0.05, medium:0.10, high:0.20} per risk; clamp[0,1]. Deterministic so AC-3 corpus-mutation moves it; distinct from confidence_score.
glossary.match_score_range: All scores (match_score, confidence, confidence_score, feedback_score, grounding_score) are in [0,1]; sentiment in [-1,1]. Pinned here (JSON Schemas omit numeric bounds to stay structured-output-safe).
glossary.seed_corpus: system/seed/ holds a committed minimal corpus: employees.json (>=6 with ids E001.., skills, availability), reviews.json (peer/manager/self per employee with ids R001..), company/case-study/job docs for the public tenant, and project_requirements samples. AC-3/AC-4 mutation tests mutate THIS deterministic corpus.
glossary.review_pause: After this freeze, fan-out is BLOCKED until human/second-agent review of the contract.
